name: Pineland Bank
description_short: Community bank operating since 1939 with 9 locations across Georgia and Florida, offering personal/business banking, loans, and deposit accounts.
description_long: Pineland Bank was originally chartered as Alma Exchange Bank & Trust on September 30, 1939, in Alma, Georgia, following the conversion of Alma Exchange Credit Union (established 1930) into a state-chartered financial institution. The bank has operated as a community banking institution for over 80 years, maintaining a presence in smaller towns across the Southeast. Pineland Bank operates 9 locations, 6 of which are full-service branches, situated in Alma, Baxley, Douglas, Kingsland, Metter, and St. Marys in Georgia, as well as Fernandina Beach, Florida. The bank is regulated under NMLS# 462015. The institution offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking services. Personal banking includes deposit accounts (checking and savings options), personal loans, mortgages, credit and debit cards, and e-banking services. Business customers have access to commercial deposit accounts, commercial loans, SBA loans, and other business services. Both segments benefit from mobile banking applications available on Google Play and the App Store, online banking access, and digital payment services including Zelle. The bank also operates Pineland Investment Services as a separate division. Pineland Bank distinguishes itself through its long-standing community banking model, emphasizing expedited processing for small business loan requests and positioning itself as a local alternative to larger national banks. The bank maintains active social media presence and provides accessibility features on its website. As a traditional community bank with limited branch presence, Pineland Bank's reach is geographically restricted to specific markets in Georgia and northern Florida. The website provides basic service descriptions but lacks detailed information about specific rates, terms, loan limits, or account features that would enable comprehensive comparison with other banking institutions.
services: Personal checking and savings deposit accounts
Investment services through Pineland Investment Services
pros: Long-established institution with 80+ years of operating history since 1939
Full-service branches in 6 locations with extended reach to 9 total locations across Georgia and Florida
Expedited small business loan processing for faster approval turnaround
Full suite of services including personal loans, mortgages, SBA loans, and commercial banking
Mobile banking app available on both Google Play and App Store platforms
Digital payment integration including Zelle for fund transfers
Local community banking focus with investment services division (Pineland Investment Services)
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to 9 locations in Georgia and Florida only
Website lacks specific information about APRs, loan terms, minimum balances, or account fees
No pricing transparency or rate comparison tools available online
Limited details about credit card rewards or specific deposit account features
No information about whether accounts are FDIC insured or insurance coverage limits
best_for: Residents of Alma, Baxley, Douglas, Kingsland, Metter, or St. Marys, Georgia seeking local banking relationships
Small business owners in covered service areas needing expedited loan processing
Customers in Fernandina Beach, Florida seeking community banking with local presence
Individuals seeking comprehensive banking including mortgages, personal loans, and investment services
diagnosis: Pineland Bank is best for customers in its 9-location service area (Georgia and northern Florida) seeking comprehensive community banking with a 80+ year track record. The main caveat is severe geographic limitation—the bank serves only specific small towns and is not accessible to most consumers, and critical rate/fee information is unavailable online.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
